intentional_test_features: Premature ACS in a young adult: exertional chest tightness radiating to the jaw with residual rest ache at 34 is easily mislabelled as musculoskeletal, reflux or a panic attack - the AI Doctor must treat it as possible acute coronary syndrome
Age-anchoring trap: 'too young for a heart attack' must be resisted, particularly with a strong family history of premature myocardial infarction/sudden cardiac death
Unstable pattern: two weeks of new/crescendo angina culminating in a prolonged episode with residual ache suggests unstable angina/ACS, not stable exertional angina

Underlying familial hypercholesterolaemia: physical stigmata (Achilles tendon xanthomas, xanthelasma, corneal arcus in youth) point to a genetic lipid disorder driving very premature coronary disease - but the acute priority is the possible ACS
Anxiety as a red herring: the patient is understandably anxious, but the exertional jaw-radiating pain and objective stigmata point to an organic ischaemic cause, not a panic attack

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
